name: Python File I/O Assembly
description: Assemble common Python file-reading and text-processing lines.

questions.question: Assemble the code to read all text from file object f.
questions.explanation: f.read() reads the entire file content.

questions.question: Assemble the code to read one line from file object f.
Assemble the code to read all lines from file object f.
questions.explanation: f.readlines() returns a list of lines.

questions.question: Assemble the loop header to iterate through each line in file f.
questions.explanation: for line in f: iterates over each line.

questions.question: Assemble the code to remove trailing newline and spaces from line.
questions.explanation: line.strip() trims surrounding whitespace, including newline.

questions.question: Assemble the code to split a line into words by whitespace.
questions.explanation: line.split() creates a list of words.

questions.question: Assemble the code to join words with a single space.
Assemble a condition that checks if 'error' appears in line.
questions.explanation: Use membership with in to test substring presence.

questions.question: Assemble the code to print the current line variable.
questions.explanation: print(line) writes the line to standard output.

questions.question: Assemble the code to store all lines in variable lines.
questions.explanation: Assigning f.readlines() stores all lines in a list.

questions.question: Assemble the code to convert stripped line text into an integer.
questions.explanation: int(line.strip()) parses a numeric string after trimming.

questions.question: Assemble the condition to detect an empty or whitespace-only line.
questions.explanation: If strip() returns '', the line is empty/whitespace.

questions.question: Assemble the statement to increment count by one.
Assemble the code to append a stripped line to list lines.
questions.explanation: Append the cleaned line using lines.append(line.strip()).

questions.question: Assemble the code to initialize lines as an empty list.
questions.explanation: Either [] or list() creates an empty list.
